researchSources: public: https://self-degree.com/ live browser surface on 2026-07-04
assumption: product is education/self-directed learning based on name and URL
assumption: visitors include learners, career switchers, founders, and skeptical buyers

audienceSegments.name: Career switcher
audienceSegments.evidence: Assumption from education/self-degree category; needs credible path and outcome proof.
jobs: understand what skill path is offered

audienceSegments.name: Self-directed learner
audienceSegments.evidence: Assumption from product name; self-learners compare structure and momentum.
jobs: check whether it fits current level

audienceSegments.name: Parent or sponsor
audienceSegments.evidence: Education products often have buyers who are not the learner; this is marked as assumption.
jobs: decide if it is worth paying for

audienceSegments.name: Busy founder learning a practical skill
audienceSegments.evidence: Assumption: founders use self-guided learning when speed matters.

masterPlan.reason: Curiosity and skepticism should meet before either turns vague doubts into exit.
A serious parent and impatient founder can sharpen each other's sense of credibility.
A course-wary switcher needs structure pressure from someone who values examples.
Playful caution and founder curiosity may make the offer feel less abstract.
Their almost-meeting should expose whether the path is clear without social rescue.
A playful parent and skeptical founder can quickly surface weak promises.
Guarded hope and curriculum skepticism should collide before commitment grows.
Curious seriousness and practical ambition can reveal whether the next step feels real.
A guarded switcher and impatient learner should force clarity on depth and pace.
Their missed timing should show whether credibility survives without peer validation.

ideas: show a concrete sample learning path immediately, with the first lesson visible before any signup.
